tldr: Move from Persona's broad identity platform to Pliance when the priority shifts to EU/Nordic AML — sanctions, PEP, CDD, and ongoing monitoring with GDPR-aligned hosting.
why_switch: Need deeper AML coverage (sanctions, PEP, CDD, ongoing monitoring) than IDV-first tooling
EU/Nordic regulatory fit and GDPR-aligned hosting matter more than US-style flexibility
Want a developer-first AML API with detailed docs and SDKs
Consolidating screening/monitoring under one Nordic provider
Custom EU-fintech contract may suit your volumes better than per-verification pricing
what_to_export: Verified user records with KYC status and timestamps
Document/biometric outcomes (metadata, not necessarily raw artifacts)
Decisioning rules currently configured in Persona
Operator audit logs and case notes
Mapping between Persona inquiry IDs and your internal user IDs
export_method: Persona supports API-based export of identity verification data per their documented data-portability story; use it to extract verification records, decisions, and audit logs. Treat PII as production-sensitive and coordinate with your DPO on retention and lawful basis for transferring data to a new processor. Capture rules and thresholds as a written spec rather than attempting a literal port.
import_method: Provision a Pliance tenant per their official documentation, configure jurisdictions and screening lists, and load customers into CDD/screening via their documented APIs. Re-screen all migrated users against sanctions/PEP/adverse-media to set a fresh baseline, then enable ongoing monitoring. Decide whether you still need a separate IDV/document/biometric vendor alongside Pliance — Pliance is AML-focused, not IDV-focused.

concept_mapping.note: Different scope; you may keep an IDV vendor alongside Pliance
Model is different; map entities deliberately
Get a quote based on expected volumes
Re-implement event handlers; new payload schema
Validate every region you serve before cutover

gotchas: Pliance is AML-focused, not a full IDV replacement — you may need a doc/biometric vendor alongside
EU/Nordic strength comes with thinner US coverage; verify per region
PII transfer between processors needs DPO sign-off and updated DPAs
Re-screening on import surfaces new alerts — staff case management before cutover
Custom pricing means TCO comparison only becomes real after a quote
Audit-trail continuity for regulators: keep Persona archives for the retention window

email.subject: Persona -> Pliance migration plan (EU AML focus)
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing we shift our compliance stack from Persona to Pliance, with a focus on EU/Nordic AML — sanctions, PEP, CDD, and ongoing monitoring.

Plan:
1. DPO/legal review of PII transfer and updated DPAs.
2. Export verification records, decisions, and audit logs from Persona per their docs.
3. Document rules/thresholds as a spec (not a literal port).
4. Stand up Pliance per their official docs; load CDD data, configure screening lists.
5. Re-screen migrated users to establish a baseline; enable ongoing monitoring; wire alerts into case management.
6. Decide if we keep an IDV/document vendor alongside Pliance for non-AML checks.

Effort 4-10 weeks. Pricing custom — we'll need a quote.

Thanks
